preamble: The diphthongization of a high nasalized front vowel [ĩ] to [ai] or [ãi] is a known historical stage in some languages before further lowering or shift, as seen in the development of long high vowels in nasal contexts.

examples.note: Historical stage where Latin long /i:/ nasalized and diphthongized towards [ãi] before lowering to [ɛ̃].

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
